title: Receipt OCR Examples
summary: Real SROIE receipt text crops with ground-truth labels show the input variation behind the benchmark.

title: How TrOCR Runs
summary: TrOCR uses a Vision Transformer encoder and an autoregressive text decoder, which makes decode length and batch composition important.

title: Controlled Batch Scaling
summary: Repeated-image batches isolate batch-size scaling from input variation and show where throughput begins to saturate.

title: Random Real-Data Batches
summary: Random SROIE batches expose straggler behavior and rising tail latency on varied OCR inputs.

title: Cost-Aware Batching
summary: Grouping samples by similar text length improves throughput and lowers estimated GPU cost per 1k images.

title: What Makes a Batch Slow
summary: Per-batch scatter plots relate latency to generated token count and ground-truth text length.

title: Profiler Call Trees
summary: Interactive call trees show CPU ops, CUDA runtime calls, and GPU stream work for representative batches.

title: Conclusion
summary: Smarter request scheduling can cut estimated GPU cost without changing the model.
